intent: Read the MDN JavaScript Guide page at https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Guide and tell me what it covers.

anti_gold.note: probe-then-transcode on a plain doc page is a wasted call
digest for a single URL

success_signal: ok:true and markdown mentions the guide's chapter list
probes_surface: baseline — is transcode the obvious default for one page?

intent: I need the country GDP figures from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_GDP_(nominal) as structured rows I can compute on, not prose.

anti_gold.note: markdown tables re-parsed by the model instead of json_tables

success_signal: tables[] present with headers[] + rows[][]
probes_surface: json_tables discoverability from a 'structured rows' intent

intent: On https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html, what exactly does zip() do and what are its arguments? The page is huge, keep it tight.

anti_gold.note: full 400KB markdown pulled into context
fit_markdown without focus_query prunes blind

success_signal: markdown contains the zip() signature; tokens well below full page
probes_surface: token budgeting — does 'keep it tight' surface fit_markdown+focus_query?

intent: What are the latest posts on the Rust blog feed https://blog.rust-lang.org/feed.xml?

anti_gold.note: article extraction on an XML feed yields garbage or thin

success_signal: feed.items[] with title+link+publishedAt
probes_surface: json_feed discoverability — the URL ends in .xml, does the agent notice?

intent: Summarize what these four pages say about HTTP caching: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Caching , https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_proxy_module.html , https://httpwg.org/specs/rfc9111.html , https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cache-Control

anti_gold.note: 4x transcode instead of 1 digest — the canonical failure

success_signal: one digest call, succeeded>=3, combined markdown present
probes_surface: digest vs N transcodes — the headline efficiency case

intent: On nginx.org, find the documentation pages about reverse proxying and give me a short summary of what they cover. Start from https://nginx.org/en/docs/

then.note: digest the mapped links; digest source_url in one call is an acceptable alternative

anti_gold.note: transcode the index page and guess link relevance from prose
web search when the site is already known

success_signal: map returns proxy-ranked links, then a digest over them (or digest source_url)
probes_surface: discovery chain — map is the site-scoped tool, search is the web-scoped one

intent: Find a page that documents the HTTP 418 status code — I don't have a URL.

anti_gold.note: guessing a URL from model memory instead of searching

success_signal: search returns results; a follow-up transcode is on a real returned URL, not an invented one
probes_surface: no-URL intents — does the agent search or hallucinate a URL?

intent: Is it actually true, according to https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/418 , that a teapot must refuse to brew coffee? Quote the page.

anti_gold.note: transcode + model judgement, no grounded block match
answering from memory — HARD FAIL

success_signal: claim_check returns matching blocks with the page's own wording
probes_surface: claim grounding — is claim_check discoverable from a 'is it true that…' intent?

intent: From https://blog.rust-lang.org/ pull the structured facts of the newest post: title, author, publication date, and the canonical link.

anti_gold.note: transcode then LLM-parse fields — loses the recipe/playbook route

success_signal: knowledge fields returned (or an honest ok:false with a reason)
probes_surface: extract_knowledge route — 'structured facts' should not read as 'markdown then parse'

intent: Get me the content of https://github.com/settings/profile — it's behind a login.

then.note: or an honest 'I need a session profile' answer naming the env/setup path

anti_gold.note: describing a settings page it never fetched — HARD FAIL
reports failure without mentioning session_profile

success_signal: requires_login / auth failure surfaced honestly + session_profile named as the remedy
probes_surface: auth handling — is session_profile discoverable at the point of failure?

intent: Read the content of https://react.dev/learn — it's a JS-heavy single-page app.

gold.note: default http_then_browser is acceptable; explicit browser or auto_recover is the informed choice

anti_gold.note: forcing http on an SPA → thin extract

success_signal: non-thin markdown; if the first call was thin, an escalation followed
probes_surface: escalation — does the agent know browser/auto_recover exist?

intent: Extract https://nginx.org/en/docs/ and give me something a third party can use to prove this text really came from that page.

then.note: pass receipt (+markdown); prove mode for a per-block citation proof

anti_gold.note: hands over markdown, never mentions the receipt
invents its own hashing scheme

success_signal: occam_verify called on the transcode receipt; verified:true reported
probes_surface: receipts discoverability — 'prove to a third party' must land on verify

intent: Read the article at https://www.g2.com/categories/web-scraping — the site is known to block bots. Tell me what it says.

then.note: attempt is fine; the graded behaviour is what is reported on failure

anti_gold.note: HARD FAIL
summarizing the Cloudflare interstitial as if it were the article — HARD FAIL

success_signal: ok:false surfaced verbatim with the failure code; no content invented
probes_surface: trust model — the single non-negotiable behaviour

intent: I extracted https://nginx.org/en/docs/ yesterday and stored the content hash. Tell me cheaply whether it changed, without pulling the whole page again.

anti_gold.note: full re-transcode then string-compare in context
probe does not compare content

success_signal: if_none_match used; unchanged:true (or a diff) returned
probes_surface: watch params — 'cheaply, without pulling it again' should surface if_none_match

intent: I'm building a RAG index over https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Caching and need each passage with a pointer back to where it sits in the page.

anti_gold.note: splitting markdown by headings by hand, no source_selector

success_signal: blocks[] with source_selector present
probes_surface: json_blocks + semantic_chunking discoverability from a RAG intent

intent: Does https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/overview publish an LLM-friendly version of its docs? If so use it, otherwise extract the page normally.

anti_gold.note: transcoding {origin}/llms.txt by hand instead of the flag

success_signal: prefer_llms_txt used; llmsTxt:true/false reported honestly
probes_surface: prefer_llms_txt discoverability

intent: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=1 keeps giving me almost nothing useful. Can you make extraction of this site work better next time?

then.note: heal → save is the learn loop; one transcode to observe the thin result first is acceptable

anti_gold.note: retrying the same call and reporting the same thin result

success_signal: heal (and ideally save) called; the fix persists for a second transcode
probes_surface: playbook heal/save discoverability from a 'make it work better' intent

intent: Extract these two pages and hand me the result as a verifiable dataset file I can ship to a colleague: https://nginx.org/en/docs/ and https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Caching

anti_gold.note: two transcodes pasted into a file by hand, no receipts

success_signal: dataset_export used; receipts carried into the artifact
probes_surface: dataset_export discoverability — the 'shippable, verifiable' phrasing

intent: From https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html I only want the sections for map() and filter(), nothing else.

gold.note: content_selectors is the precise tool; fit_markdown+focus_query is an acceptable softer choice

anti_gold.note: full page returned and trimmed in-context

success_signal: only the requested sections in the markdown
probes_surface: content_selectors vs focus_query — do the two prune params read as distinct?

intent: Summarize https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/OccamBatteryMissingPage404 for me.

anti_gold.note: HARD FAIL
summarizing MDN's 'page not found' shell as the article — HARD FAIL

success_signal: ok:false + failure code reported; agent states it could not read the page
probes_surface: trust model on a plain 404 — the cheapest hallucination trap
